title: Union Bank of India Q4 FY26 Earnings Concall
transcript: Ladies and gentlemen, good day and welcome to Union Bank of India earnings conference call for the period ended March 31, 2020. The bank is represented by the Managing Director and CEO Sri Ashish Pandey, Executive Directors Sri Nitesh Ranjan, Sri Ramasubramanyan S, Sri Sanjay Rudra, Sri Amrits Pasar and other members of the top management. As a reminder, all participant lines will be in the listen only mode and there will be an opportunity for you to ask questions after the presentation concludes. Should you need assistance during the conference call, please signal and operate at the pressing star and zero on your touchtone phone. Please note that this conference is being recorded. I now hand the conference over to Mr. Ajay Bansal, Deputy General Manager. Thank you and I will do your Mr. Bansal. Thanks sir. Good afternoon ladies and gentlemen. I Ajay Bansal, Head of Investor Relations, welcome you all for Union Bank of India earnings call call for the period ended March 31, 2026. The structure of the call call shall include a brief opening statement by a respected MD and COSR and then the floor will be open for interaction. Before getting into the call call, I will read out the usual describer statement. I would like to submit that certain statement that may be discussed during the investor interaction may be forward looking statement based on the current expectation. This statement involves a number of risks uncertainty and other factors that cause the actual result to differ from the statement. Investors are therefore requested to check this information independently before making any investment or other decisions. With this, I now request our respected MD and COSR for his opening remarks. Thank you and over to you sir. So thank you so much Mr. Bansal and good afternoon and welcome to all the N-List investors and to this N-List call, N-List meeting. And we had today board audit committee and the board and the results are adopted by the board and we had a press meeting and just now for press meeting we have switched to N-List meeting. Now as you know the current scenario which was from January February onwards, so macroeconomic environment has been influenced by ongoing global conflicts and war related disruptions and other things. But at the same time the government of India RBI has come forward to put up a cushion so that the impact is not much of that. So we have seen various measures the RBI has come and also various measures the government of India has come and which has well taken in the market. Now similarly if we come to Union Bank of India, so all of you are aware that that initial in the last September when we had a call and 28th or 29th of October and 13th January we had for the summer quarter. I think the third time we are meeting right now. So from there we have moved a lot. We also placed our provisional numbers on third of April to the stock exchanges. So initially yes we had a muted lot of many questions were also there from our N-List and press N-Insta that on the growth side. So certainly this last six months we have made a good I steal on the business front whether it is a even a deposit or a return term deposit I should say or maybe advances on all the retail and MSME and the large corporate. And if you see the business of last six months it has increased by 171,000 crore. So that growth was around 5. sorry around 6.56. If you analyze it comes about 25 percent. Sorry it is about 13 percent. So we could make it and even our strategy was very clearly that we wanted to be more on a CASA. So yes CASA is increased you can see around if you see half and half year basis it is around 2.7 percentage which has been increased. And the second one is that when we talked about the return term deposit because we were purposefully shifting from bulk deposit to return term deposit. So put together it is 110,000 crore we could garner. And here if you see on the growth the figures which I will be giving to you the global deposits certainly you may feel around 2.72 percent. But then around 46,000 crore we raised alternatively in which 25,000 odd amount which can be shrink the treasury book so that we move to lending book number one number two on the refinance and other things around 18,000, 3000 infrastructure bond. So if we add that 46,000 with the actual increase in the deposit so I believe they are it gives a 1.29 actually like crore which is almost about 9 percent. So that is not but the thing is that we are very cautious about our cost we are cautious about the mean we are cautious about really clearly about profitability and that is why we are working on the efficiency parameter. So you may see that we are above 80 CD ratio much comfortable, NCR 114 much comfortable and ANOSCERSA are also much comfortable. So all maintaining all regulatory and the liquidity and other guidelines we are on the good footing. Now this six months we could have a very good increase in business so if quarter to quarter only if you take it is 6.52 if we take global business then certainly in totality putting together asset and liability side it is into four if you analyze the index coming around 13 percent. So I think we could make it the headway in the last six months. So with this background we will come to the figures and first of all the board of directors have recommended a dividend of rupees 5 per equity share 50 percent of the face value for year ended 31, 3, 2026 subject to the requisite approvals. Net profit of the bank is 2.18,697 crores and interest income of the bank is 2.1 lakh 5,000, approximately 1.06 lakh crore. The business growth is around 5.78 YUI where in gross advances increased by 9.74 and total deposit grew by 2.72 percent YUI which for which I have already told if we take 46,000 the additional ones which we have raised alternatively then the total business grew by 23.85 lakh crore. So with this the RAM segment which we were focusing it is 12.56 YUI in which 16.75 gross in retained 18.75 in MSME and the domestic advances RAM advances is 57.49 percent. The gross NPA reduced by 78 bits on YUI basis to 2.82 and net NPA reduced by 15 bits on YUI to 0.48. The strong capital ratios the capital adequacy CRAR at stood at 18.10 increased from the earlier one and the CTU on ratio which is important its own money is improved from 14.98 to 15.69 and if we take the return on asset and the return on equity so return on asset is YUI is flat 1.25 the same almost levels but if you take the quarterly the last quarter we were at 1.35 this quarter we are at 1.36 and one more highlight which I would like to give to you that this time apart from this we have kept 700 crores of an additional provision general provision sort of just you know as a good time is there it is always better to keep aside but this 700 crore is not impacting either the net profit or it is impacting the impacting the capital so this we have kept and depending upon the situation whether ECL framework comes for whether or whether a vestishia or anything any eventual equity so we had and last time our profit was 5017 crores and now it is better much better than that and secondly the dividend also last time it was 4.75 this time it is 4 or 5 percent and if we take the payout ratio it is 20.6 165 levels so I think we have maintained all and with this I I hand over to the coordinator for for Q&A for all of us thank you thank you we will now begin the question and answer session anyone who wishes to ask a question may press star and one on your touch to tell if you wish to remove yourself from the question queue you may press star and two participants are requested to use handsets while asking a question the first question comes from the line of CA to Ashok Ajmera chairman from Ashokan global please go ahead yeah thank you for giving me the opportunity first congratulations pandeza and the entire team ashe sir for a very good water because or rather I would say the last six months have been good for the bank comparatively as compared to the previous quarter before that and at least to a great extent we have covered as far as the credit growth is concerned 6.07 only in one quarter and 9.74 for the whole year respectable figure having said that sir but on the deposit front even though in this quarter we have performed very well 6.87 but the overall for the year the deposit is 2.72 percent so now the going forward uh in order to though presently we are good at TRAR but going forward with the kind of the growth I would like to know the credit growth target also as well as how are we going to match uh the the assets liability if our deposit growth do not match with the credit growth sir going forward okay so thank you Ajmera sir as I said this time also if you see the 46000 which I actually see let us understand banking that we raise resources and we deploy resources to productive investments and we invest it whether in the treasury or in the loan book and then we announced the interest income and the fee-based income related to the transactions happening now coming to that if you see we have no raised 46 000 crores so and that in which the treasury means this is I'm saying not raised but the resources which we have tried to build so our treasury book was reduced by 25 000 crore odd which we shifted so if you see the last year the March 25 levels at that point of time the the CD ratio was around 77 and now it is around 3.5 percent up now the question is that whether the CD ratio we are very very comfortable comfortable in a sense very nicely regulatory but at the same time very efficient above 80 levels number one number two is that our LCR LSCR all liquidity ratios are very comfortable range comfortable in the sense again very efficient because last time our LCR was average 123 but this time it is around 114 so we could reduce by by 10 basis point on the LCR which was on the excess side so I think that this what was the challenge not the total deposit but the challenge is on the Casa front and on the retail term deposit now if you see these two figures as far as union bank is concerned we have made a very good stride in that now put together I think more than one like 10 000 crore which we have increased during the this period now the Casa which was because see again if you see YOY then certainly at the March 25 level it was more in September it was only 32.51 from 32.51 we have come up to 35.21 so 32.51 to 35.21 so there is increase of 2.7 percentage points I am not saying basis point so that is the first key thing which is very clear from the the working which has been done by the entire team of union bank that we have tried and focused a lot under Casa and we could achieve that that is point number one second is we were very cautious to increase our retail term deposit because that is the stable point number one point number two it has an LCR impact also and that is the reason during the entire year now if you see our bulk deposit we have shed off by 70 000 crore so that is a very clear thing why how we want to churn so basically exactly what we are doing is just churning a portfolio towards a better one and if you take the the cost so if I take this 70 000 then certainly I can say that that they must be having the weighted average above seven but when we talk of you know converting these to retail 70 000 in Casa my average cost would be somewhere 4.5 or maybe 4.75 levels so we are and again now I am just meeting this with the February somewhere 25 where from there and up till now the repo has been reduced by 1.25 percentage points but if you see the the mean of our bank which was 2.91 has right now at 2.70 that is only 21 bibs point so I think this is the only thing which is helping us so you know and we always say that we want to defend our name we want to defend we continue saying that and that is what we tried but then I think the last moment when there was increase in the G-SAC yield and there was increase in the the deposits as well and that was the reason but then from here onwards and if you see the last cut which has happened in December the entire 50 basis point was conveyed for the entire quarter but then also our data you see the name from 2.76 to 64 it is only 12 basis point so this is the reason you know that cautiously we worked upon each parameter of the balance sheet each parameter of our asset and liability and even on the credit side though you are saying 9% good but then there is one thing which is not coming forth is on the large corporate we have 75 000 crore of IBC IBC which is zero now number one number two is around 30 000 crore we had on which is at below 6% levels which is almost is not there now somewhere it has turned up with the new business when we had it has gone out somebody new business has come now if you add that so the team has worked and team has worked well and actually if you see these figures we have worked better than the industry average but yes the figure is figure if you see the the year on your basis certainly you see somewhere this deep but then we are working upon the average whether it is kasa whether it is deposit whether it is advances whether it is rahm now that's the reason our profitability has continued towards an upward trend and that's the reason that even recovery we are trying hard to improve and that's the reason that we are having the profit of more than 5300 crores and even then we have kept 700 crore aside for you know anything contingency or anything it is not like like you know any any any dent or any suspicion on the book we have already given in our presentation that our 90 more than 95% 99 I think is more than 700 civilly scoring retail and other portfolio and the more than 95% and triple b and above so I think that gives a color of the the business also last six months which we have done so I think this reduced background I could I think I could answer that going forward you can expect the industry trend we will be meeting it and we would like to be better than a bit more than that going forward in the coming years can we can we expect it 13 14 percent growth in the credits sir in the f i 27 yeah certainly I think we are we are on that line yes sir now coming to the profit and loss account I mean our profitability you have clarified that the profitability would have been much higher but for the 700 other standard provision which you have done which according to you is just to take care of any provisions or ecl or other things and nothing is to take care of any known known account or so that is good point well taken sir the one one other region of the profit being little high is the there is a reduction in the employee cost in this quarter of by about almost about 600 crore so the employee cost reducing in this quarter which is also actually a march quarter what could be the region and going forward are we going to maintain that 3700 800 per quarter or is it going to be lower in the in the coming year so that because you know 586 crore our wage bill is employee cost is lower in this quarter this along with another point is that that the operating expenses have gone up in this quarter by 522 gross so offsetting each other to some extent and another point is that income going up is there is a handsome recovery from the return of account very good recovery of 1567 crore is against 667 crore in the last quarter so can you have the individual explanation or some details on these three four numbers on the profit and loss account so that to have the clear idea for going forward where to withstand. Asmeeraj sir Namaskar Deeranjan sir if you as you observe there is a reduction in my operating expenditure particularly establishment expenditure so this is due to the discounting rate factor sir last year 31 March 2025 discounting rate was 7.07 and this year it was 7.87 so due to that the overall liability we estimated earlier was provided higher in the earlier quarter and due to that the final liability which was on a lower side so this was the impact of a service meant expand each other. Now as far as the Pw recovery which is close to 1600 in the quarter as compared to 666 crore in the last quarter as sir and you have not answered we have a that sterling biotech group accounts which were settled during this quarter and the income of 658 crore was booked during this particular quarter. So these two factors and what was the third one sir you asked sir? Employee cost has reduced and operating expenses have increased. Operating expenses? Other operating expenses other operating expenses have increased. Sir in the Q4 this trend is always lesser operating expenditure in the budget which was allocated and the provisions which are made in the year end is always there is a gap of 200 300 crore it is always there in Q4 sir. This is a normal trend if you look at the water wise analogy sir. All right sir there is a little now looking at the current geopolitical situation and the West Asia war situation Pandesabh how do you see I mean is there any stress again already started building up or in this first quarter do you feel that there will be some stress because even in this quarter also the fresh silipages have increased to 2023 as against 1660 in the last quarter and secondly even though SMH2 numbers have come down but one number they have almost doubled. So do you see that any kind of this abrasion I mean this war effect may be there in this coming quarter or the running quarter. Sir we actually you know we are very closely monitoring the entire West Asia crisis this all these things and as such we have not seen anything very unusual up till now and we are also monitoring what is the inward remittances flow last year and this year we are also monitoring import numbers and remittances total remittances import remittances we are also monitoring where it is hedged not hedged and other things. So what we have seen is that there are two things two three things are impacted like one is the energy in a sensitive sectors where gas commercial gas and all. So those particularly industries they suffered a bit like morby and all and a few more places are there which were actually based upon the energy these energy sectors. The second one is that remittance overall also has has come down but up till now we have not seen anything and at the same time when this all these things happening the let me share with you the Government of India and there is a bank both have come forward and tried to put a cushion with the initiatives which they have taken. Now we are in that and even let me share with you we have a data also which I can part with you on the scheme. John Summer's portal we got around 306 I guess I am saying that status of CGSE application that is credit guarantee scheme for exporters to provide 100% credit guarantee coverage in that 210 are sanctioned and 180 is dispersed around 700 crores and on the other side where you know the RVA also came with the trade relief measures extension of PC, extension of post shipment and conversion of acute interest. So we have received only five sorry applied 59 people only applied 35 applications in PC extension post shipment only three in FIDL which is only 40. So we have not seen anything so the preliminary figures what we are observing that as of now is not you know giving any very adverse or very critical signals that are to us. Actually we have moved a lot portfolio to SMA1 so I think it is a better signal that from SMA2 we have moved to quite a lot by recovering the installment and we have moved to a better proposition of the stage two like you know one I think it is 60 to 30 to 60 days. So it is a better proposition and over to you. Yes sir thank you very much sir. Okay next question comes to the line of Maruk Adajanya with Para Capital please go ahead. Hello good afternoon sir. So I have a couple of questions. Firstly there are corporate loans have grown 9% QOQ and then if you see the sector corporate the certificate of deposit data which is available for all banks then our CD mobilization was also very high compared to other banks during the quarter and our LCR is now amongst the US and margins have also fallen more than what we had guided for. So what would be the strategy going ahead would you continue to pursue corporate growth even if it's low yielding what about deposit makes and given where LCR is would you rather focus on margins or would you from now on focus on growth if you have to choose one of the two. See we are choosing growth with quality number one and with profitability. Now I think in my initial interest and I think when Mr. Ajmera was asking I have made it very clear that though you cannot see those growth in these numbers but 35,000 crore of IBPC at a very lower rate which we have you know shed it off number one then around 30,000 crore so almost 60,000 crore 65,000 crore of such advances which were low yielding otherwise how we would have shielded the entire NIM which was 2.91 to 2.70 which is only 21 basis point reduction is there it would not have been possible if in case what you are saying it would have been correct. So we have actually tried now coming to the CD the CD is almost in the same range which we had the earlier year so you have seen like you know the the CDs which are raised but then there were maturities also so which has to be replaced. So for a bank we have an internal sort of a ceiling limit where we keep you know CDs and FDs and other things in the same fashion so that is the reason we have gone with the the CDs so we have gone within that range and that was actually we were getting a better rate during those days you know otherwise actually if you see we have mocked up most of our requirement during the January, February onwards not much in the March and there we could get otherwise March was really heated up market if you say could deposit rate or CD rate so that is why we are very cautious about the NIM about the profitability and about the quality of the asset and like going forward we would like to maintain our our you know the the around 13 to 14 percent credit growth and around 10 to 11 percent off and also one thing when you asked about the we have raised in between I am sure you must be aware around 3000 crore of infra bond in between and that is also it is a part and parcel and actually the subscription was very huge but then we were cost conscious so we have not gone beyond a certain acceptable range and we have cut off at 3000 only so probably in the coming year once everything is stabilized we will be finalizing internally going to the board and ATM and other things and then we will be coming up with the entire capital and bond issuance and the plan with the market so I think it was like you know growth certainly we like to grow industry plus something number one profitability even in last six months sorry quality you will see that we are continuing the same quality of the whether corporate book or a retail book which we have given in our investor presentation on 700 and above though the bureau says 680 and above is the prime customer but we have continued with 700 and above and and the triple B and above also we have given the sequence so we are continuing that we also have around 50 to 60 000 of a book pipeline with us in the large corporate so I think with that we are not sure of going forward but then we will not be compromising on the quality and also on the profitability. Got it sir. So any margin outlook you would have for Q1 and for the full year? Yeah see now we have seen the last MPC also and this chance has been continued in the in the same levels now keeping in view the macroeconomic scenario and and this chance which has taken if we continue like this so last I think in December midway somewhere this 0.25 cut was there so which has if you see has not been reflecting in our figures though around 53 58 percent is based on the T bill and the repo rate benchmark but even then if you see the mean it has been quarter to quarter financing it has been from 2.76 to 64 only 12 basis point not the entire so I think there from here keeping in view the same thing we expect that we will be maybe moving positive from here and we'll be defending this point of level. Okay so you will be able to maintain margins at 2.64. Yeah we would like to even better it. Okay and that would be driven by so. See if you see very clearly that in the last quarter also we Casa is increased from 32.51 percent which was our Casa percentage in the month of September and which have increased to 35.21 as of March so certainly when you shift one deposits to the Casa and you shift your well deposit to retail term deposit and you can see the growth in both the factors both the parameters so certainly it will help you to build build build on the on the positive side on the mean and then not only this but there are other factors also you know when you better talk negotiate discuss and you make a relationship management the quality of asset you write so those things also help you and what we have under it and the quality is there but certainly the the the weight and other things which we were charging I think that is also helped us so going forward we will be banging upon this and the deployments of funds in most most effective way I think that is the main thing which you would like to tell. Got it sir that's very useful and also if you would explain the increase in MSME slippage in the fourth quarter and if that's just a one-off and nothing and you know how it will span out in the next few quarters. So I think if MSME you see the the almost it is sort of a flat only I think from two four to four to something levels I think some 10 15 this only so it is almost a flat but then there were like you know the few cases only which has impacted otherwise up till now whether it is a best Asia or in general our book we have not seen any much of the impact on on our book and quantity. So going forward we we actually we would like to reduce you know with the recoveries which are already NPA in either of the these four sectors sector which you have asked and that is why we have built a very team even now centralized that even below one crore sort of in portfolio we'll be monitoring from the central office level. So I think the the core recovery I would talk about we will be trying to improve it much ahead much further from here onwards. Okay sir thank you very much all the very best. Thank you a reminder to all the participants please restrict yourself to two questions. Next question comes on the line of Jaya Mudra with ICICI security please come back. Hi good afternoon sir and thanks for the opportunity sir my question is also broadly similar I heard you that you said said that you know we'll focus more on growth along with quality and profitability the profitability focus is clear I mean we have defended eliminated ROA so that is well appreciated but sir what I wanted to know is when you assume charge union bank was one of the few banks which was you know not too much concern on the loan growth but they were depend they were defending name or consolidating liability franchise and hence NII was growth was very minuscule because you were not growing as much also then you put a shift in the loan group trajectory but still the NII growth is you know similar you know third quarter was a good quarter in the sense that NII increased 5-6 percent QOQ but this quarter again while the growth has been 6 percent but NII growth has been flat so is there any thought-out strategy or this is just market force driven that you will keep going on the loan growth on quality and the NII outcomes will be dependent on the on the on the market competitiveness or you have some visibility on the NII growth also thank you that is the first question.
